question: What architectural paradigm does the 5G Core (5GC) use, replacing the point-to-point architecture of 4G EPC?
What protocol is used for user plane data tunneling in the 5G Core?
What is a PDU Session in the 5G Core?
options: A logical connection between the UE and a Data Network providing IP connectivity
A physical radio bearer between the UE and gNB
A management session between the SMF and AMF
A security tunnel between the UE and UPF
A signaling session between the gNB and the AMF
correct_answers: A logical connection between the UE and a Data Network providing IP connectivity

question: What identifier is used in the 5G QoS framework to mark and differentiate QoS flows?
In the 5G QoS model, what is the mapping relationship between QoS flows and radio bearers?
options: Multiple QoS flows can be mapped to a single radio bearer
One QoS flow maps to exactly one radio bearer always
Radio bearers are not used in 5G; only QoS flows exist
Each radio bearer must carry exactly one QoS flow
QoS flows are terminated at the gNB and not carried over radio
correct_answers: Multiple QoS flows can be mapped to a single radio bearer

question: What is Network Slicing in 5G?
options: The ability to create multiple logical networks over a shared physical infrastructure, each tailored to specific use cases
The division of the radio spectrum into frequency slices for different operators
A technique to reduce interference by slicing the cell into sectors
The partitioning of the core network into control and user planes
A method to slice bandwidth between subscribers on a shared bearer
correct_answers: The ability to create multiple logical networks over a shared physical infrastructure, each tailored to specific use cases

question: Which 5G Core Network Functions are involved in PDU session establishment? Choose all that apply.
Which 5G use case benefits most from network slicing with a dedicated low-latency slice?
What is the role of the UPF (User Plane Function) in the 5G Core?
options: Anchor point for user plane data, performs packet routing and forwarding
Manages radio resource allocation at the gNB
correct_answers: Anchor point for user plane data, performs packet routing and forwarding

question: Which of the following correctly describes the 5G Service-Based Architecture (SBA)? Choose all that apply.
options: Network Functions communicate via standardized service-based interfaces
Each Network Function exposes services that other NFs can consume
SBA uses RESTful HTTP/2-based APIs between Network Functions
NFs are tightly coupled and communicate only with predetermined partners
SBA enables flexibility and modularity in deploying network functions
correct_answers: Network Functions communicate via standardized service-based interfaces
Each Network Function exposes services that other NFs can consume
SBA uses RESTful HTTP/2-based APIs between Network Functions
SBA enables flexibility and modularity in deploying network functions
